SEARCH
Module com.examplez.demo
Package com.examplez.demo.Controller

Class PlayController

java.lang.Object
com.examplez.demo.Controller.PlayController
public class PlayController extends Object
FXML controller for the active card-game view (game-view.fxml).

Coordinates the JavaFX interface with the Game model, renders the human player's hand and the discard pile, manages human card selection, and runs the turn loop for human and machine players on a background task.

All interface changes made from the background task are delegated to the JavaFX Application Thread through Platform.runLater(Runnable).

See Also:
  • Game
  • PlayerHuman
  • PlayerMachine

  • Constructor Details

    • PlayController

      public PlayController()

  • Method Details

    • setStage

      public void setStage(javafx.stage.Stage stage)
      Stores the primary application stage for later scene changes.
      Parameters:
      stage - application stage that hosts the game

    • initialize

      public void initialize(int numberOfPlayers) throws InterruptedException
      Initializes the board for the selected number of players.

      The method updates player visibility, collects the card image views from the FXML container, and starts a new game session.

      Parameters:
      numberOfPlayers - total number of human and machine players
      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if game startup is interrupted

    • startGame

      protected void startGame() throws InterruptedException
      Creates the game model, deals the initial cards, renders the starting interface, and launches the turn-management task.
      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if startup is interrupted

    • createThreadTurnTask

      protected void createThreadTurnTask()
      Creates and starts the daemon thread that executes the turn loop.

    • controlTurnTask

      protected javafx.concurrent.Task<Void> controlTurnTask()
      Builds the background task that controls successive player turns until only one participant remains.

      Human turns wait for a card-selection event, while machine turns are processed automatically after a short randomized delay. Eliminated players are removed from the model and hidden from the interface.

      Returns:
      a task containing the complete turn-control loop

    • showHandCardPlayer

      protected void showHandCardPlayer()
      Displays the current human hand by resolving each card's image resource and attaching its click handler.

    • addListener

      protected void addListener(javafx.scene.image.ImageView imageView, Card card)
      Registers the click action for one card image.

      The card is processed only while the human player's turn flag is active.

      Parameters:
      imageView - image node associated with the card
      card - model card represented by the image

    • showCardPile

      protected void showCardPile()
      Displays the last played card and refreshes the accumulated score.

    • playCard

      protected void playCard(Card card)
      Attempts to play a card selected by the human player.

      Valid ace cards may require the player to choose between the values 1 and 10. After a valid play, the hand, pile, and round state are updated. Invalid cards produce a feedback message instead.

      Parameters:
      card - card selected from the human player's hand

    • eliminatePlayer

      protected void eliminatePlayer(int turnPlayer)
      Removes a player from the game and hides the corresponding interface container.
      Parameters:
      turnPlayer - turn identifier of the player to eliminate

    • askAceValue

      protected int askAceValue()

    • changeView

      protected void changeView(int idWinner) throws IOException
      Loads the final view and passes the winner identifier to its controller.
      Parameters:
      idWinner - turn identifier of the last remaining player
      Throws:
      IOException - if final-view.fxml cannot be loaded

    • updateBoard

      protected void updateBoard()
      Shows or hides machine-player containers according to the selected number of participants. The managed state is changed together with the visible state so hidden containers do not occupy layout space.
